The following code runs as expected:
```py
import  numpy as np
string_array = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
numpy_arr=np.array(string_array)
```
However, when I swap NumPy for CuPy like this:
```py
string_array = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]

# Convert to a CuPy array
cupy_array = cp.array(string_array)
```
I encounter the following error:
```
 return _core.array(obj, dtype, copy, order, subok, ndmin, blocking)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "cupy\_core\core.pyx", line 2408, in cupy._core.core.array
  File "cupy\_core\core.pyx", line 2435, in cupy._core.core.array
  File "cupy\_core\core.pyx", line 2574, in cupy._core.core._array_default
ValueError: Unsupported dtype <U6
```
Is it possible to create a CuPy array in a similar way? Thank you in advance for your help!
Thanks in advance


### To Reproduce

```py
string_array = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]

# Convert to a CuPy array
cupy_array = cp.array(string_array)
```


### Installation

None
